subject: Project Management
title: The Interstate Highway System
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Interstate Highway System.

hook.text: Eisenhower signed the Highway Act in 1956. It took 35 years and $530 billion to finish.

buildup.text: The plan called for 41,000 miles of highway connecting every major American city.

discovery.text: Eisenhower's inspiration was Germany's Autobahn — he saw how fast armies moved on highways.

twist.text: The highways displaced 475,000 people and fueled urban sprawl and car dependency.

climax.text: America's largest public works project reshaped the nation — for better and worse.

punchline.text: The road project that connected America also divided it.

quiz.question: What inspired Eisenhower to build the Interstate Highway System?
options: Germany's Autobahn and its military value
A desire to reduce train travel
